title: Crime and Punishment
author: Dostoyevsky, Fyodor
summary: Rodion Raskolnikov, a proud but impoverished ex-student in St. Petersburg, wanders feverishly through squalid streets, tormented by isolation, hypochondria, and daring fantasies. Crushed by debt and conscience, he broods, rehearsing a rash exploit and fretting over trivial details that might betray him. He avoids his landlady, notes the city’s filth and stench, and obsesses about conspicuous rags and a ruined hat. Driven to a nervous blankness, he makes his way to the pawnbroker’s tenement for a tentative reconnaissance. There he meets a wary, withered old woman who admits him into a dim room lit by the setting sun silently waiting.
